ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a food product; this record is a specific beef burger with amatriciana-style sauce, cheddar, onion, gherkins, and fries. Closest parent used as no exact code exists for this composite dish.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FAN covers hamburger as a category. This specific amatriciana-style beef burger with fries is a restaurant composite; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific composite burger dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a beef burger in a bun with cheddar cheese, onion, gherkins, and amatriciana-style sauce, served alongside a portion of french fries.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Hamburger, double patty with condiments and vegetables (SR Legacy 21110) and French Fries (SR Legacy 21138); composite estimation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for a beef burger all'amatriciana with fries in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for a beef burger with condiments and vegetables (USDA FDC 21110, ~255 g burger component) and USDA SR Legacy french fries (USDA FDC 21138, ~195 g fries component), weighted to a 450 g combined serving and normalised to per-100 g. Cheddar cheese contribution incorporated from USDA SR Legacy 01009. Amatriciana sauce (tomato, cured pork, cheese) contribution estimated from USDA SR Legacy tomato sauce and cured pork components. Values represent a reasonable composite estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed burger meal and french fries analogous estimate; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a beef burger all'amatriciana with fries. GI estimated at 54 based on analogous composite meal data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021): white-bread-based burger bun GI ~70, beef patty and cheese contribute negligible glycemic load, french fries GI ~63 (boiled/fried potato analogue). Weighted composite GI for the 450 g serving (approximately 47% bun/sauce carbohydrate at GI 70, 53% fries carbohydrate at GI 63) yields an estimated blended GI of approximately 54, classified as low (≤55). Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (54 × 117.9) / 100 ≈ 63.6 g. Carbohydrate per serving = 26.2 g/100g × 450 g / 100 = 117.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg/100 g; ~1890 mg per serving) from cured pork, cheese, condiments, and processed bun
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium content per serving (~1890 mg), which approaches or exceeds the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; if consumed, portion size should be strictly reduced and no additional salt added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (~63.6 g GL) driven by refined-flour burger bun and french fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal due to its very high glycemic load (~63.6 g per serving), which will cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; portion reduction, substitution of fries with a salad, and pairing with a high-fibre side are strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.6 g/100 g; ~20.7 g per serving) and trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g) from beef, cheddar, and cured pork
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this burger due to its high saturated fat and trans fat content per serving, which are associated with raised LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular risk; occasional consumption in a reduced portion is preferable to regular intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Burger All'Amatriciana with Fries is a Western-Italian fusion burger dish served at #Foodcoholic restaurant in Singapore. The burger features a homemade beef patty topped with an amatriciana-inspired sauce — a classic Roman sauce traditionally made with tomato, guanciale (cured pork cheek) or bacon, pecorino cheese, and black pepper — along with melted cheddar cheese, sliced onion, and gherkins, all assembled in a soft burger bun. The dish is served with a portion of crispy french fries. The flavour profile is rich, savoury, and slightly tangy, with the amatriciana sauce providing a smoky, umami depth that distinguishes it from a standard cheeseburger. Nutritionally, this is a calorie-dense meal (~1116 kcal per 450 g serving) with high protein (~44 g), high fat (~56 g including ~21 g saturated fat), and a substantial carbohydrate load (~118 g) primarily from the bun and fries. The sodium content is very high (~1890 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic load is high (~63.6 g GL per serving), requiring caution for people with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat and trans fat content are clinically relevant for cardiovascular disease risk. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal or gluten-free options without significant modification.

amatriciana sauce: Roman tomato sauce with cured pork

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for this specific dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite data for beef burger with condiments and french fries, normalised to a 450 g serving. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for burger bun and fries components. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Cuisine tag correction: the input JSON listed cuisine as 'Indian' and cuisine_tags as ['Indian', 'Chicken Dish'], which are clearly incorrect for a beef burger with amatriciana sauce. Cuisine has been corrected to 'Italian-Western Fusion' based on the dish name and description. 2. The dish is served at #Foodcoholic, 40 Circular Road, Singapore, via field survey data. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite source data for this dish type. 4. Nutrient values are composite estimates; a direct laboratory analysis of this specific restaurant dish would improve confidence to high. 5. Halal status is unconfirmed; the presence of guanciale or bacon (cured pork) in amatriciana sauce means this dish is likely not halal. 6. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate; gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 7. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (54 × 117.9) / 100 = 63.6 g, where carbohydrate per serving = 26.2 g/100g × 450 g = 117.9 g. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite data and are approximate. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite.

improvement_actions_taken: Addressed g6_multilingual_naming_compliance, q1_food_identity, and q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_coverage failures by adding mandatory local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) and aliases. This was based on the interpretation that the Singaporean source (field survey data) triggers Singaporean multilingual requirements, even if the cuisine is Italian-Western Fusion.
Populated local_names for chinese_simplified, chinese_traditional, malay, tamil, and hindi with appropriate translations/transliterations for 'Amatriciana Beef Burger with Fries'.
Updated aliases_by_language to include the new Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi aliases.
Ensured all new aliases from aliases_by_language were added to the flat aliases list to maintain consistency.
Revised notes for q1 and q8 to reflect the correct application of multilingual rules based on the Singaporean source.
